headline: AI models nail SPY rally but split on Tesla ahead of earnings
summary: All five AI models correctly predicted SPY's rise to $711.21, driven by Trump's Iran ceasefire extension easing geopolitical tensions. Tesla proved divisive with models split 3-2 on direction ahead of Q1 earnings, while NVDA confounded bearish calls by holding above $202 despite competitive pressure from the Marvell-Google partnership news.

consensus_picks.note: Perfect consensus as all five models correctly called SPY's rise with similar confidence levels around 0.65.
Three bearish models were wrong as TSLA held at $387.51 despite earnings concerns, while GPT-4o's bullish call proved correct.
Bullish models prevailed over Claude's bearish view, correctly predicting NVDA would hold above $202 despite competitive threats.
Both models correctly but tentatively called Bitcoin's modest rise to $78,577 with low 0.5 confidence.

best_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence MRVL call capitalized perfectly on the Google chip partnership news catalyst for the day's best score.

worst_call.summary: Claude's bearish TSLA call with 0.72 confidence backfired as the stock rose despite pending earnings, marking the day's worst prediction.
